rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim is present but loosely framed; 'make it stronger' plausibly requires sharpening the thesis (what PINNBench solves that no prior benchmark does) and the gap statement motivating its construction.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening a technical argument centrally involves tightening inferential links — e.g., connecting the composite metric S to the stated design goals, and grounding the claim that 'adaptive collocation dominates' with explicit conditions.
rationale.R1.4: A stronger benchmark paper must articulate its novelty delta against nearest prior PINN benchmarks; the artifact currently omits any comparison to existing evaluation suites, making novelty assessment a live and central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: The prose is clear but flat; 'make it stronger' most commonly means more precise, impactful sentence construction — reducing hedges, tightening definitions, and elevating the register of technical claims.

parsed.clarification_question: Should strengthening focus primarily on prose quality and sentence-level impact (R1.6 becomes load-bearing), or on tightening the logical argument and novelty positioning against prior PINN benchmarks (R1.2 and R1.4 become load-bearing)?
